headlines.title: Trump loyalty tests, major battlegrounds and more: What to watch in Tuesday’s elections
headlines.description: President Donald Trump’s campaign to suppress dissent in his own party faces its most prominent test yet as he takes aim at Rep. Thomas Massie in Kentucky, while GOP candidates across the country look to leverage the president’s endorsement in key primaries.

headlines.title: Justice Department agrees not to pursue any tax claims against Trump as part of IRS deal
headlines.description: The addendum comes a day after the Justice Department announced that Trump and his co-plaintiffs would drop their $10 billion suit against the IRS.

headlines.title: Investigators looking into manifesto left by San Diego shooting suspects
headlines.description: Investigators say they are looking into a 75-page manifesto left by the suspects in the San Diego mosque shooting. The documents, which authorities say are filled with extremist material against minorities and praise the shooter in the Christchurch massacre in New Zealand, were found in the suspects' vehicle. NBC News correspondent Tom Winter outlines some of the details of the documents, which NBC News has reviewed.

headlines.title: American Doctor Infected With Ebola While Working in Congo
headlines.description: The CDC confirms an American doctor has tested positive for the deadly Ebola virus while working in the Democratic Republic of Congo. This comes as entry into the United States is being restricted for some travelers in response to the outbreak. NBC’s Molly Hunter reports for TODAY.

headlines.title: Arsenal win Premier League title for first time in 22 years
headlines.description: Arsenal’s two-decade absence atop the Premier League has ended, giving one of the world’s biggest and richest clubs the title it had long sought but narrowly missed for years.

headlines.title: Trump talks Iran while giving tour of White House ballroom construction
headlines.description: President Trump took questions from the press about the conflict with Iran after giving a tour of the White House ballroom construction site. NBC News' Gabe Gutierrez reports on the president's decision not to strike Iran and ballroom developments.

headlines.title: Senate advances resolution to end Iran war as GOP Sen. Bill Cassidy flips to support it
headlines.description: The Senate voted to move forward with a resolution to force President Donald Trump to end the war in Iran, a breakthrough for the Democratic-led effort.

headlines.title: DOJ official told GOP ally that big payouts were coming for Jan. 6 defendants
headlines.description: Months before the $1.7 billion fund was announced, Justice Department official Ed Martin said Capitol rioters would get millions, even if it took until 2028.

headlines.title: As Meta lays off 10%, 7,000 employees will be moved into AI roles, source says
headlines.description: Meta is moving 7,000 employees into roles focused on AI and consolidating them into four new organizations, according to a source familiar with the matter.

headlines.title: Woman dies in NYC after stepping into open manhole
headlines.description: A woman fell to her death in New York City after she accidentally stepped into an open manhole, authorities said.

headlines.title: What to watch for tonight in Thomas Massie’s unique district: From the Politics Desk
headlines.description: Steve Kornacki dives into the key places to watch in Rep. Thomas Massie’s district tonight as he tries to fend off a GOP primary challenger backed by President Donald Trump.

headlines.title: Justice Department agrees not to pursue any tax claims against Trump as part of IRS deal
headlines.description: The addendum comes a day after the Justice Department announced that Trump and his co-plaintiffs would drop their $10 billion suit against the IRS.
